originalTitle: ‘No offence detected’: Victoria police close investigation into Moira Deeming allegation of assault by Matthew Guy
summary: Guy accuses Deeming of making ‘ridiculous and unsubstantiated’ claims as state Liberal party grapples with fallout
content: Police have concluded their investigation into Victorian Liberal MP Moira Deeming’s allegation that her colleague Matthew Guy assaulted her, making the finding “no offence detected”. Deeming had reported to police on 16 June that Guy, the opposition’s public transport spokesperson, had assaulted her at a Macedonian community event in Sunshine on 23 May. Sources close to Guy on Thursday said he vehemently denied the allegation and was considering a defamation legal action against Deeming. In comments to the Age after the police investigation ended, Guy said: “These claims were ridiculous and unsubstantiated, just as the police found”. Sign up for the Breaking News Australia email In a statement issued late on Thursday night, police said they had “concluded an investigation following reports of an assault at a function venue on Sommerville Road in Sunshine West on 23 May.” “It was reported a woman was assaulted by a man at an event,” police said. “The woman did not require medical attention, and the incident was reported to police on 16 June. “Following a thorough investigation, it has been determined there was no offence detected. “Investigators have engaged with the woman who made the report.” Both Deeming and Guy have been approached for comment. The opposition leader, Jess Wilson, had refused to comment on the allegations while the investigation was under way. Speaking on ABC Radio Mildura on Friday morning, Wilson thanked police for their “prompt” investigation but she would wait for Guy to address media before providing further comment. “I’m pleased that the police investigation has concluded promptly, and that no offence has been detected,” Wilson said. “I have given Matthew that opportunity this morning to respond. I’ve reached out to Moira to seek a conversation with her when she returns from overseas.” Guy was expected to make a statement to media on Friday morning, with Wilson to hold a press conference in Mildura in the afternoon. The allegation – just five months out from the election – has heaped pressure on Wilson, who, after her appointment as leader in November, had managed to bring stability to the Liberal party, which has endured years of infighting between the moderate and more conservative groupings. The party also faces a serious challenge from a surging One Nation, which risks its chances to form a majority government. Wilson last week embarked on a five-week tour of each of the state’s 88 electorates, to pitch the Coalition as ready to govern. More details soon …
chineseTitle: “未发现任何犯罪行为”：维多利亚警方正在密切调查莫伊拉·迪明对马修·盖伊的袭击指控
chineseSummary: 盖伊指责迪明在州自由党努力应对后果时提出“荒谬且未经证实”的主张
chineseContent: 警方已经结束了对维多利亚州自由党议员莫伊拉·迪明（Moira Deeming）指控她的同事马修·盖伊（Matthew Guy）袭击她的调查，调查结果“没有发现任何犯罪行为”。迪明于 6 月 16 日向警方报告称，反对派公共交通发言人盖伊于 5 月 23 日在阳光城的一次马其顿社区活动中袭击了她。周四，接近盖伊的消息人士称，盖伊强烈否认这一指控，并正在考虑对迪明提起诽谤法律诉讼。警方调查结束后，盖伊在接受《时代报》采访时表示：“正如警方所发现的那样，这些说法是荒谬且未经证实的”。在周四深夜发布的一份声明中，警方表示，他们“在收到 5 月 23 日阳光西区萨默维尔路一个活动场所发生袭击事件的报告后，已完成调查”。警方表示：“据报道，一名妇女在一次活动中遭到一名男子的袭击。” “该女子不需要医疗护理，该事件已于 6 月 16 日向警方报告。“经过彻底调查，已确定没有发现任何犯罪行为。 “调查人员已与举报的女士进行了接触。”我们已联系迪明和盖伊征求意见。在调查进行期间，反对党领袖杰西·威尔逊拒绝对这些指控发表评论。周五早上，威尔逊在美国广播公司米尔迪拉电台发表讲话时感谢警方“及时”调查，但她会等待盖伊向媒体发表讲话，然后再发表进一步评论。威尔逊说：“我很高兴警方的调查迅速结束，并且没有发现任何犯罪行为。” “今天早上我给了马修回应的机会。我已经联系莫伊拉寻求对话
whyItMatters: 这条信息属于全球新闻信号，适合用于观察外部环境、政策变化、地缘风险和市场情绪。
